description:
**Milestone:** Phase 5: Enterprise Features

Define 18-month LTS support windows with security backport process. Build automated migration tooling from Shoelace, Carbon, and Material UI to Helix. CEM-driven analysis of source library + automated wrapper generation.

**Files to Modify:**
- packages/cli/src/commands/migrate.ts
- docs/

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] LTS policy documented (18-month support window)
- [ ] Security backport process defined
- [ ] Migration CLI: helix migrate --from shoelace
- [ ] CEM-driven source analysis for migration
- [ ] Migration guides for top 3 competitor libraries

**Complexity:** large
title: Establish LTS release process and migration tooling

lastReviewFeedback:
coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 6**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

````
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@docs/lts-policy.md`:
- Around line 22-30: The markdown fences in the LTS policy doc lack explicit
fence languages and have no blank lines around them, triggering MD040/MD031;
update the unlabeled fenced blocks (the timeline/text block, the git example
block, and the lts list/text block referenced in the diff) to include
appropriate languages (e.g., ```text for timeline and LTS list, ```bash for the
git commands) and ensure there is a blank line before and after each fenced code
block so markdownlint passes; locate the fenced sections by the visible content
("Release date ... Active LTS", the "git checkout -b security/..." snippet, and
the "lts/1.x — LTS for Helix v1" block) and apply the changes consistently to
the other mentioned ranges (lines 54-57, 64-68).

In `@docs/migration/migrate-from-carbon.md`:
- Around line 12-18: The fenced code block in the list item for "Run automated
migration analysis" is not surrounded by blank lines (violates markdownlint
MD031); edit the list item containing the triple-backtick block shown and insert
a blank line immediately before the opening ``` and another blank line
immediately after the closing ``` so the code fence is separated from the
surrounding list text and passes MD031.

In `@docs/migration/migrate-from-shoelace.md`:
- Around line 7-17: The fenced code blocks under the numbered list items (the
npm install and npx helix examples) violate markdownlint MD031 by lacking blank
lines around the fenced blocks; update the two list items so there is a blank
line before the opening ``` and a blank line after the closing ``` for each
fenced code block to ensure proper spacing within the numbered list.

In `@packages/cli/src/commands/migrate.ts`:
- Around line 167-230: The built-in mapping constants (CARBON

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: lts-policy.md:30 is an ASCII art timeline diagram. This is intentional documentation design — it renders correctly in standard Markdown renderers (GitHub, Starlight). A "⚠️ Minor" bot flag on documentation formatting doesn't justify changing a deliberately constructed visual aid. No correctness issue, purely cosmetic preference.

migrate-from-carbon.md:18 is the CEM path `./node_modules/@carbon/web-components/custom-elements.json`. This is accurate for the published @carbon/web-components package. It's a documentation guide, not executed code. The path is presented as an example the user can adapt. Minor bot flag on a valid documentation example doesn't warrant a change.

migrate-from-shoelace.md:17 is the CEM path for Shoelace. Same reasoning as the Carbon path — this is a documentation example that accurately reflects the Shoelace package structure. Minor bot feedback on a correct documentation path is not worth the churn.

Line 230 is the closing of CARBON_MAP. The map includes `cds-button` and `cds-text-input` for Carbon v11 parity but is missing `cds-checkbox` and `cds-select`. This is a real gap — Carbon v11 uses `cds-` prefix for all components, so any project on v11 using `cds-checkbox` or `cds-select` would get "no mapping defined" from the CLI instead of a useful migration guide. Major gap in migration coverage.

Line 497 is `writeFileSync(wrapperFile, wrapperContent, 'utf-8')` — and lines 504/509 have the same pattern. All three writeFileSync calls are unguarded. On permission denial, non-existent directories, or read-only filesystems, Node.js throws an uncaught exception with a raw stack trace instead of a user-friendly error message. For a CLI tool this is a Critical UX/reliability issue. The fix is straightforward: wrap file writes in try/catch and print a clean error message.

Line 533 is in the arg parsing loop inside parseMigrateArgs. Unknown flags are silently ignored — if a user typos `--ouput foo` or `--frm shoelace`, the CLI silently proceeds with wrong/missing values. For `--from`, we get the "missing required flag" error later. But for `--output` or `--cem` typos, the CLI runs with no output file and no warning. Adding an unknown-arg warning improves debuggability with minimal effort.
